chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

text: 3151 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «حَدُّ مَا حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ مِنَ اَلْمَدِينَةِ مِنْ رَبَابٍ إِلَى وَاقِمٍ وَ اَلْعُرَيْضِ وَ اَلنَّقْبِ مِنْ قِبَلِ مَكَّةَ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3151 - Abu Basir narrates from Abu Abdullah (as):
"The boundaries of what the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) declared sacred from Medina are from Rabab to Waqim, and from al-‘Urays to an-Naqb, in the direction of Mecca."
